home.comingSoonTitle: Video Lessons & Community Are on the Way
home.comingSoonText: We're working hard to bring you engaging video lessons and a supportive community space, making menstrual health education even more interactive, social, and fun.
home.ctaText: Join thousands of girls taking control of their menstrual health through education.

about.heroDescription: We make learning about your body easy, safe, and even fun. No awkwardness, no judgment, just real answers and a friendly AI guide (hi, that's Sakhi!) to help you every step of the way.
about.ourStoryP1: Aarohi was born from a simple yet powerful realization - millions of students lack access to accurate menstrual health education. Misinformation, stigma, and cultural taboos leave young minds confused and anxious about a natural biological process.
about.ourStoryP2: We set out to change that. By combining AI-powered guidance with culturally sensitive, age-appropriate content, Aarohi creates a safe space where students can learn, ask questions freely, and grow with confidence.
about.whyAarohiSub: Designed to make menstrual health education accessible, engaging, and stigma-free for every student.
about.learnWithConfidenceDesc: Curated, age-appropriate lessons that explain menstrual health clearly and accurately - no confusion, no shame.
about.aiSakhiGuidanceDesc: Your personal AI companion who answers every question with patience, empathy, and scientifically accurate information.
about.breakStigmaDesc: Normalizing conversations around menstrual health through open dialogue, accurate facts, and a supportive community.
about.inclusiveLearningDesc: Designed for every student regardless of background - multilingual, accessible, and welcoming to all identities.
about.ourValuesSub: The principles that guide everything we create.
about.empathyDesc: Understanding every unique journey with compassion
about.trustDesc: Reliable information reviewed by health experts
about.privacyDesc: Your data stays yours, always safe and secure
about.inclusivityDesc: Safe, welcoming space for students of all backgrounds
about.howWeHelpSub: Four pillars that make menstrual health education effective and empowering.
about.interactiveLessonsDesc: Engaging, multimedia lessons designed to make learning about menstrual health feel natural, interesting, and empowering. Each module builds knowledge step by step.
about.aiSakhiDesc: An AI companion available 24/7 to answer any question - no judgment, no embarrassment. Sakhi provides accurate, age-appropriate responses with warmth and empathy.
about.mythsVsFactsDesc: We tackle common misconceptions head-on with clear, evidence-based facts. Our myth-busting approach helps students separate cultural beliefs from medical reality.
about.trustedResourcesDesc: Every piece of content is reviewed by health professionals and aligned with WHO guidelines. Students and parents can trust the information they find on Aarohi.
about.ourVisionText: We envision a world where every student grows up with accurate knowledge about their body, where menstrual health is discussed openly without shame, and where AI-powered education bridges the gap between curiosity and understanding. Aarohi is building a future where menstruation is just a normal part of life, not a secret.
about.ourImpactSub: Built with purpose, designed for impact.
about.learningModulesDesc: Curated lessons covering menstrual health, hygiene, and body literacy
about.aiGuidanceDesc: Sakhi is always available to answer questions with empathy and accuracy
about.multilingualSupportDesc: Breaking language barriers to reach students across diverse communities
about.safeAndPrivateDesc: Your privacy is our priority. Learn in a secure, judgment-free space
about.quoteText: Education is the first step toward breaking stigma.

lesson.question: Question {{current}} of {{total}}
lesson.glossaryUterus: The uterus (womb) is a hollow, pear-shaped organ where a baby grows during pregnancy.
lesson.glossaryCervix: The cervix is the lower, narrow part of the uterus that opens into the vagina.
lesson.glossaryOvulation: Ovulation is when an egg is released from the ovary, usually around day 14 of a 28-day cycle.
lesson.glossaryMenstruation: Menstruation is the monthly shedding of the uterine lining, commonly known as a period.
lesson.glossaryUterineLining: The uterine lining (endometrium) thickens each cycle to prepare for a possible pregnancy and is shed during your period.
lesson.glossaryEstrogen: Estrogen is a hormone that helps regulate the menstrual cycle and is important for reproductive health.
lesson.glossaryProgesterone: Progesterone is a hormone that helps prepare the uterus for pregnancy.
lesson.glossaryHormones: Hormones are chemical messengers in your body that help regulate processes like your menstrual cycle, mood, and energy.
lesson.glossaryCramps: Menstrual cramps are caused by the uterus contracting to help shed its lining.
lesson.glossaryMenstrualCup: A menstrual cup is a reusable, flexible cup worn inside the vagina to collect menstrual blood.
lesson.glossaryFollicularPhase: The follicular phase is the first half of your cycle, from the first day of your period until ovulation.
lesson.glossaryLutealPhase: The luteal phase is the second half of your cycle, after ovulation until your next period.

resources.heroDesc: Turns out, most period "facts" are just myths with good PR. Let's fix that.

askSakhi.suggestion4: Can I exercise during my period?
